summary: Phase 0 SMIFS Wealth-Engagement Agent fully verified end-to-end. 8/8 backend pytest cases pass and all 12 frontend Playwright assertions pass. /api/health reports llm_reachable=true with model gemma-4-E4B. /api/chat creates sessions, persists history in MongoDB, and the LLM correctly recalls prior-turn facts (name 'Aakash' recalled). Empty message returns 422. /api/conversations/{sid} returns persisted messages without _id leak. /api/docs and /api/openapi.json return 200. UI loads with the premium navy (rgb(11,27,43)) + gold theme, Cormorant Garamond serif heading, suggestion chips, typing indicator, and persists smifs_session_id in localStorage across reloads. Reset-thread clears state correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); migrate to lifespan handlers in a future iteration (non-blocking).

chat_with_fallback iterates the full MODEL_CANDIDATES chain on every request which incurs ~3 wasted Hub AI HTTP calls (gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o, claude-3-5-sonnet → 403) before falling back to 'auto'. Consider caching the last-successful model for the process lifetime to cut latency from ~2-3s to ~700ms.

Frontend does not fetch /api/conversations/{sid} on mount, so a refresh shows an empty thread even though the session is preserved server-side. This matches the stated spec ('messages from prior turns are NOT re-rendered automatically') but is worth surfacing as a minor UX gap for future work.
No rate-limiting / abuse protection on /api/chat (Phase 0 scope, but flagging for Phase 1).
test_credentials: N/A — Phase 0 has no auth. LLMHUB_API_KEY already configured in /app/backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: Tests created throwaway conversation sessions in the 'conversations' MongoDB collection (random UUIDs). No fixed seed data needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_smifs_chat.py covers health, docs, chat (new session + memory + 422 validation) and conversations (GET + 404). Hub AI 'auto' route → gemma-4-E4B works; 403s on gpt-4o/claude in backend logs are expected and handled by fallback. Frontend testids confirmed: smifs-chat-shell, smifs-title, health-pill, welcome-card, suggestion-{0..2}, chat-input, send-button, typing-indicator, msg-user-N, msg-assistant-N, session-id, reset-thread. localStorage key is 'smifs_session_id'.
